title: berteroa incana
intro: Berteroa incana, also known as Hoary Alison or Hoary Ball Cottongrass, is a flowering plant species in the family Brassicaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis, and its expectorant properties help relieve congestion. The plant's leaves and flowers are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, contributing to its health benefits, which include reducing inflammation and improving immune function. Berteroa incana can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its dried leaves and flowers are often used in traditional medicine to promote respiratory health and alleviate coughs.
